Old doc error Cited 151_copilot_cli/codex-rs/ paths No such tree — corrected in WP49

Korean corpus (reference)

Verbatim KO corpus 014_web-search/14_cp_web-search.md for cross-check; code blocks unchanged.

Copilot CLI — Web Search 구현

분석 스냅샷: 151_copilot_cli/npm-package/ (@github/copilot@1.0.7) + deobfuscated/ readable chunks. Copilot CLI is not a codex-rs checkout in this corpus — web search is implemented in the bundled TypeScript runtime.

Copilot CLI exposes web research through three cooperating mechanisms: (1) OpenAI Responses hosted web_search when the active model advertises webSearch: true, (2) GitHub MCP github-mcp-server-web_search aliased to web_search as fallback, and (3) a first-party web_fetch URL reader with markdown simplification.

Architecture

flowchart TD
  Agent["Copilot agent session"] --> Route{"model.webSearch === true?"}
  Route -->|yes| Hosted["Responses API web_search"]
  Route -->|no| MCP["github-mcp-server-web_search → rename web_search"]
  Agent --> Fetch["web_fetch local tool"]
  MCP --> GH["GitHub MCP X-MCP-Toolsets: web_search"]
  Fetch --> Ler["Ler() fetch + turndown HTML→markdown"]

1. Dual-path web_search

Tool list assembly (C9t)

When building the MCP tool list for a session, Copilot removes the GitHub MCP search tool if the model already supports native hosted search:

// deobfuscated/.../slash-json-api-repos-copilot-embeddings-code-search-prompt-agent-examples.js:1146-1157
function C9t(t, items, n) {
  let r = items.findIndex((a) => a.name === R9t),
    o = r !== -1 ? items.splice(r, 1).at(0) : undefined,
    s = o ? { ...o, name: w9t, safeForTelemetry: true } : undefined;
  (n && !v7e(n)) || (t?.webSearch !== true && s && items.push(s));
}
// R9t = "github-mcp-server-web_search", w9t = "web_search"
Condition Tool exposed Backend
model.webSearch === true Hosted web_search only OpenAI Responses API server pipeline
model.webSearch !== true Renamed MCP web_search GitHub MCP web_search toolset
v7e(settings) === false Neither web path Policy disables all web tools

Canonical minified mirror: npm-package/app.js (same C9t / R9t / w9t symbols).

Policy gate (v7e)

// deobfuscated/.../keyword-engine__0617.js:2206-2208
function v7e(t) {
  return !li(t, "copilot_swe_agent_disable_web_tools");
}

When copilot_swe_agent_disable_web_tools is active, C9t skips injecting MCP web search and web_fetch registration is also suppressed (store/reindex.js:1734 uses v7e(settings) ? msr(d) : undefined).

2. GitHub MCP web_search integration

Toolset request (client-registry.js)

Default GitHub MCP headers include web_search in allowed toolsets:

// deobfuscated/src/app/mcp/host/client-registry.js:21-31
let e = "repos,issues,users,pull_requests,code_security,secret_protection,actions,web_search";
// ...
"X-MCP-Toolsets": e,
"X-MCP-Host": "github-coding-agent",

Built-in MCP server lists web_search among GitHub tools (keyword-engine__1223.js:66).

Rename rationale

MCP tool arrives as github-mcp-server-web_search (namespaced). Copilot renames to web_search with safeForTelemetry: true so the agent sees a stable tool id matching hosted search and UI labels.

3. Hosted web_search (Responses API)

When t?.webSearch === true, Copilot does not inject the MCP fallback. Search executes on the OpenAI Responses server — same pipeline as Codex (121_openai-codex/codex-rs/):

  • Query decomposition → Bing + OAI-SearchBot → URL visit / passage extraction → rerank → citations

Copilot-specific surface in this snapshot is the model capability flag (webSearch) and tool-list routing above — not a separate Rust web_search.rs in 151_copilot_cli/.

For Rust ToolSpec::WebSearch, WebSearchAction, and web_search config modes (live/cached/disabled), see 014_web-search/14_co_web-search.md (shared OpenAI stack lineage).

Action types (Codex protocol reference)

Hosted search uses the same action discriminant Codex documents:

Action Purpose
search Keyword query (optional queries array)
open_page Navigate to URL
find / findInPage Pattern search within page
other Forward-compatible bucket

Copilot does not reimplement these in 151_copilot_cli/ — they are serialized by the Responses API and mapped in the shared codex-rs protocol layer when using Codex-class models.

4. web_fetch local tool

Registration (keyword-engine__0617.js)

// :2252-2266
(C7e = "web_fetch"),
(BJt = class {
  getTool() {
    return {
      name: C7e,
      title: "Fetching web content",
      description: "Fetches a URL from the internet and returns the page as either markdown or raw HTML...",
      input_schema: Js(Isr),
      callback: this.webFetch.bind(this),
      safeForTelemetry: true,
    };
  },

Parameters (Isr schema :2231-2251)

Param Default Max Purpose
url required — HTTP(S) target
max_length 5000 (dsr) 20000 (usr) Response char cap
start_index 0 — Pagination offset
raw false — Raw HTML vs markdown

Permission (webFetch :2309-2321)

When permissions.requestRequired, calls permissions.request({ kind: "url", intention: "Fetch web content", url }).

Protocol blocks

  • file: rejected — directs agent to file read tool (:2302-2308)
  • Truncation note with next start_index (:2339-2343)

HTTP fetch core (Ler)

// deobfuscated/src/app/core/slash-html-x-turndown-language--document-fragment.js:940-943
async function Ler(url, format, maxBytes, signal) {
  let o = AbortSignal.timeout(3e4),
    a = await fetch(url, { redirect: "follow", signal: s, headers: {} });
  • 30 s timeout (3e4 ms)
  • Streaming read with maxBytes cutoff (Fys = 10 * 1024 * 1024 in keyword-engine__0617.js:2228)
  • HTML → markdown via ehs() turndown path (:981-990)
  • On stream overflow appends [Content truncated: exceeded N byte limit] (slash-html-...js:970-972)

Success / failure telemetry (webFetch :2347-2377)

Success returns textResultForLlm prefixed with Contents of ${url}: and metrics originalContentLength / returnedContentLength.

Failures capture errorMessage in toolTelemetry.restrictedProperties; URL hashed as hashedUrl for telemetry while full URL kept in restricted props (:2279-2282).

4b. Session tool assembly

web_fetch is registered only when web tools are enabled:

// deobfuscated/src/app/session/store/reindex.js:1734-1742
let f = v7e(settings) ? msr(d) : undefined,
  N = [u.shellTool, ...(u.otherTools ?? []), ...p, G, g, ...h, f, ...extraTools]
    .filter((tool) => tool !== undefined),

msr(d) instantiates BJt (keyword-engine__0617.js:2209-2211). MCP web_search arrives separately via C9t during MCP tool merge — not in this array.

Order in combined tool list: shell → memory → editing → diagnostics → web_fetch → plugins/skills.

4c. Artifact verification (PLAN.md)

Fact Evidence
Canonical runtime 151_copilot_cli/npm-package/app.js
Version @github/copilot@1.0.7 (github-copilot-1.0.7.tgz)
Public repo v1.0.7 has no app source Reconstruction uses bundle deobfuscation, not codex-rs
Installed SHA match PLAN.md:22 — npm app.js SHA equals live install

WP49 citations use deobfuscated paths mapped from reconstructed_v2/modules/ chunk IDs (e.g. module 0646 = C9t, 1526 = ACP mapper).

5. UI and agent protocol mapping

Tool group (onBack-onRetry-onManual.js:15)

{ id: "web", label: "Web", tools: ["web_search", "web_fetch"] },

Status strings (keyword-engine__1526.js)

// :396-399 — web_search
let s = o && o.length > 40 ? o.slice(0, 37) + "..." : o;
return s ? `Searching for '${s}'` : "Web search";

// :380-394 — web_fetch (hostname-aware truncation)

ACP tool kind (Pki :623-628)

Tool Kind
web_search, github-mcp-server-web_search search
web_fetch fetch

Slash /help table (slash-help-theme-model-compact-server.js:151-154)

Documents web_fetch params: url, raw, max_length, start_index. Notes: pagination via start_index; no auth headers.

Allowed-tools frontmatter (slash-tips-...js:290-293)

web_search, web_fetch, websearch, webfetch map to null in Har — permission parser treats them as unrestricted category aliases.

6. Comparison: Copilot CLI vs Codex vs OpenCode

Aspect Copilot CLI (cp) Codex (co) OpenCode (oc)
Primary search Responses web_search OR GitHub MCP ToolSpec::WebSearch Rust Exa/Parallel MCP websearch
Fallback GitHub MCP rename N/A —
URL fetch web_fetch (JS) — webfetch (Effect)
Disable switch copilot_swe_agent_disable_web_tools web_search=disabled config Runtime flags
Source tree 151_copilot_cli/deobfuscated/ 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/ 141_opencode-current/

7. Security notes

web_fetch

  • Blocks file: protocol (keyword-engine__0617.js:2302)
  • URL permission prompt when requestRequired
  • 10 MB byte stream cap (Fys), 20k char return cap (usr)
  • No custom auth headers in Ler fetch (headers: {})

MCP web_search

  • Requires GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN bearer (client-registry.js:28)
  • Tool exposure gated by X-MCP-Toolsets and optional X-MCP-Exclude-Tools

Telemetry

Renamed MCP tool sets safeForTelemetry: true on the web_search alias (C9t).

web_fetch also sets safeForTelemetry: true on tool definition (keyword-engine__0617.js:2265). Telemetry uses hashed URL in properties while retaining restricted full URL for debugging (:2279-2282).

7b. Operational workflow (agent perspective)

Typical multi-step web research in Copilot CLI:

  1. Discovery — call web_search (hosted or MCP) with natural-language query; UI shows Searching for '…' (keyword-engine__1526.js:396-399).
  2. Deep read — call web_fetch on a result URL; markdown default simplifies HTML for token efficiency.
  3. Pagination — if response includes <note>Content truncated. Call the fetch tool with a start_index of N</note>, continue with start_index=N (keyword-engine__0617.js:2339-2343).
  4. Permission — first fetch to a domain may block on permissions.request until user approves (:2309-2321).

This differs from OpenCode where websearch returns MCP blob text in one shot and webfetch is a separate Effect tool — Copilot's web_fetch pagination is more explicit in the tool contract.

Reverse engineering (015)

RE

015_reverse-engineering

Overview

Reverse engineering (015) — developer-depth section from the codex corpus. Structural highlights extracted from the analysis:

  • 이 축은 설치 help parity 문서의 다음 단계다. 기존 00~14 축은 주로 help/config/local-doc 표면을 비교했고, 이 축은 설치된 바이너리와 번들에서 복원 가능한 구조를 따로 기록한다.
  • | Level | 의미 | 사용할 수 있는 주장 |
  • | installed-help | --help, public command output, version output | 명령/플래그/공개 subcommand가 존재한다 |
  • | safe-local-doc | 설치 패키지의 docs, changelog, examples, manifest | 문서화된 schema/경로/기능 설명이 있다 |
  • | bundle-key | minified bundle에 남은 module key, source path, route name, proto name | 내부 모듈/라우터/프로토/컴포넌트가 존재한다 |
  • | binary-string | native binary strings, file, otool, codesign, nm | crate/package/protocol/tool/evaluator 이름이 embedded되어 있다 |
  • | runtime-trace | synthetic project에서 실제 명령을 실행한 결과 | 동작 semantics, precedence, permission enforcement를 확인했다 |
  • | private-artifact-name | 비공개 상태 파일의 경로/개수만 관찰 | artifact가 존재한다. 내용/schema 주장은 금지 |
  • 015_reverse-engineering/33_source_deobfuscation_inventory.md further separates source-like recovery levels. In this corpus, Cursor reaches preserved module-key recovery, while Grok/Kiro/AGY mostly expose native
  • auth/token/cookie/credential/login data
  • raw transcript, chat history, conversation DB/PB
  • logs and terminal captures

Main analysis

015_reverse-engineering/00_reverse_evidence_policy.md

Reverse Engineering Evidence Policy

This axis is the next step in the installation help parity document. The existing 00 to 14 axis mainly compared the help/config/local-doc surface, and this axis separately records the structures that can be restored from installed binaries and bundles.

Evidence Levels

Level Meaning Claims available
installed-help --help, public command output, version output Command/Flag/Public subcommand exists
safe-local-doc docs, changelog, examples, manifest in the installation package There is a documented schema/path/function description
bundle-key Module key, source path, route name, proto name remaining in minified bundle Internal modules/router/prototypes/components exist
binary-string native binary strings, file, otool, codesign, nm The name crate/package/protocol/tool/evaluator is embedded
runtime-trace Results of executing actual commands in synthetic project Checked operational semantics, precedence, and permission enforcement
private-artifact-name Observe only the path/count of private state files An artifact exists. Content/schema claims are prohibited

015_reverse-engineering/33_source_deobfuscation_inventory.md further separates source-like recovery levels. In this corpus, Cursor reaches preserved module-key recovery, while Grok/Kiro/AGY mostly expose native source-path, handler, protobuf, and runtime-trace evidence. Do not call this "full source recovered" unless a real source map or readable source archive is present.

Hard Boundary

The purpose of reverse analysis is to restore product structure, not to extract user state. Next, reading the content is prohibited.

  • auth/token/cookie/credential/login data
  • raw transcript, chat history, conversation DB/PB
  • logs and terminal captures
  • browser profile state, local storage, IndexedDB, session storage
  • memory/goal runtime stores
  • screenshots and browser recordings
  • private mcp.json, settings, config values unless explicitly redacted

For private artifacts, only “exists” is used. Field/schema/content assertions are only used when there are public docs, source/bundle evidence, sanitized fixtures, and synthetic runtime traces.

Installed CLI version pin (2026-06-27)

Single source of truth for pinned ay/cr/gr/ki binaries:

  • 015_reverse-engineering/36_installed_cli_version_manifest.md

WP5 trace shards (38–41) extend hubs without changing evidence-level definitions above.

Current Upgrade Scope

2026-06-27 pass — installed CLI practical RE (goal 4e94e12d-a9b):

Prefix Target Evidence kind
ay ~/.local/bin/agy v1.0.12+ help, changelog, strings; 38_ay_project_flags_trace.md
cr cursor-agent 2026.06.26-7079533 bundle string literals, help; 37, 39_cr_automations_help_trace.md
gr grok-0.2.67-macos-aarch64 inspect JSON, strings; 40_gr_0267_inspect_refresh.md
ki Kiro CLI.app 2.5.0 help surfaces; 41_ki_help_surface_trace.md

Newly reversed on 2026-05-31 pass (historical baseline):

Prefix Target Evidence kind
gr ~/.grok/downloads/grok-0.2.14-macos-aarch64, ~/.grok/bundled, ~/.grok/docs/user-guide Rust Mach-O strings, crate paths, ACP methods, bundled agents/roles/personas/skills
cr ~/.local/share/cursor-agent/versions/2026.05.28-418efe5 Webpack module keys, route extraction, proto module names, native helper inventory
ki /Applications/Kiro CLI.app, ~/.local/bin/kiro-cli* signed universal Mach-O binaries, Rust source-path strings, WebKit resources, dashboard/autocomplete assets
ay ~/.local/bin/agy, ~/.gemini/antigravity-cli Go/native strings, protobuf trajectory/cascade names, local cache/config metadata, helper binaries

Reading Rule For Existing Docs

When a 00~14 page says “None” or “Not supported”, interpret it as “Not exposed in installed help” unless this 15_reverse-engineering axis confirms absence from bundle/binary evidence too.

When a page states runtime semantics from config/cache observation, this axis upgrades or downgrades the claim:

  • bundle-key or binary-string confirms a module exists.
  • runtime-trace is still required for exact precedence, scheduling, isolation, merge, and permission behavior.

015_reverse-engineering/25_cr_bundle_module_map.md

Cursor Agent Bundle Module Map

Current install: 37_cr_bundle_refresh_20260626.md + 36_installed_cli_version_manifest.md (2026.06.26-7079533). This page is the May 2026 baseline for webpack exports.modules recovery.

This pass inspected installed Cursor Agent JavaScript bundles under ~/.local/share/cursor-agent/versions/2026.05.28-418efe5. It did not execute model sessions, authenticate, read private Cursor data, or mutate Cursor config.

Bundle Identity

Field Evidence
Package root ~/.local/share/cursor-agent/versions/2026.05.28-418efe5
Package name @anysphere/agent-cli-runtime
Release marker agent-cli@2026.05.28-418efe5 in Sentry release strings
Source maps none present as *.map
Module recovery method Webpack exports.modules keys preserved original ./src/... paths
Recovered ./src/ module keys 408 unique keys; 479 key occurrences across chunks

Launchers and binaries:

  • cursor-agent: shell launcher
  • cursor-agent-svc: shell launcher
  • cursor-agent-svc.js: local service bundle
  • index.js: main CLI bundle
  • node: bundled Mach-O arm64 Node runtime
  • cursorsandbox: Mach-O arm64 sandbox helper
  • native addons: file_service.darwin-arm64.node, merkle-tree-napi.darwin-arm64.node, node_sqlite3.node, pty.node

Command Module Map

The bundle exposes 53 unique command modules under ./src/commands/. High-signal groups:

Group Recovered modules
Chat/session chat.ts, create-chat.ts, resume.tsx, cloud-list.ts, cloud-transcript.ts
MCP mcp-list.ts, mcp-list-tools.ts, mcp-login.ts, enable.ts, disable.ts
Sandbox sandbox-config.ts, sandbox-debug.ts
Worker worker-mode.ts, worker-pool.ts, worker-debug.ts, worker-directory.ts, worker-account-policy.ts, worker-policy-validation.ts
Auth/env login.ts, logout.ts, auth-status-format.ts, dev-login.ts, env.ts
ACP acp.ts
Search/record/status search.ts, record.ts, status.ts, repo-status.tsx, repo-info.tsx
Install/update install.ts, install-core-posix.ts, install-shell-integration.ts, update.ts, update-core.ts

This confirms the CLI command surface is not a thin wrapper around agent [prompt...]; it is a multi-command runtime with worker, MCP, sandbox, auth, cloud, and ACP subcommands.

Subagent And Collaboration Modules

Recovered modules:

  • ./src/subagent/cli-subagent-host-adapter.ts
  • ./src/subagent/subagent-prompt-handler.ts
  • ./src/subagent/subagent-session-store.ts
  • ./src/subagent/subagent-token-store.ts
  • ./src/subagent/subagent-update-router.ts
  • ./src/context/subagent-session-context.tsx
  • ./src/shared/cli-subagent-host-adapter.ts
  • generated proto import family: subagent_exec_pb.js

Implication: Cursor Agent has a real subagent host/session/update stack in the installed bundle. Public help still lacks a standalone subagent command group, so exact authoring and execution semantics remain runtime-trace pending.

MCP And Approval Modules

Recovered modules:

  • ./src/mcp/approval.tsx
  • ./src/mcp/approve-plugin-mcps-on-install.ts
  • ./src/mcp/disabled-store.ts
  • ./src/mcp/error-utils.ts
  • ./src/mcp/project-paths.ts
  • ./src/mcp/scope.ts
  • ./src/shared/lazy-mcp-lease.ts
  • UI modules for MCP tools/resources/servers under ./src/components/*mcp*

Key behavior recovered from bundle snippets:

  • User config path: ~/.cursor/mcp.json
  • Project config path: <projectRoot>/.cursor/mcp.json
  • State files: MCP approvals and disabled-store paths are derived through project-paths.ts
  • Project MCP enable flow computes approval IDs for project servers and removes disabled entries
  • OAuth callback helper listens on localhost port 8787 and verifies callback state
  • Error mapping distinguishes disabled servers, team policy blocks, and team network allowlist blocks

Sanitized runtime trace in 015_reverse-engineering/29_cr_mcp_handshake_trace.md confirms the approval path at runtime: project mcp enable writes .cursor/projects/<project-slug>/mcp-approvals.json under HOME, mcp list-tools rejects unapproved project servers before loading them, and an official stdio MCP server can be loaded enough to enumerate 14 tools.

Worker / Bridge / Service Modules

Recovered modules:

  • ./src/bridge/bridge.ts
  • ./src/bridge/bridge-client.ts
  • ./src/bridge/management-server.ts
  • ./src/bridge/worker-api-server.ts
  • ./src/bridge/proxy-http2-session-manager.ts
  • ./src/bridge/self-hosted-worker-metrics.ts
  • ./src/commands/worker-mode.ts
  • ./src/commands/worker-pool.ts
  • ./src/commands/worker-directory.ts
  • ./src/commands/worker-debug.ts

Management-server routes recovered from source-keyed bundle text:

  • /healthz
  • /readyz
  • optional /metrics

This is distinct from cursor-agent-svc.js local service routes already documented as /health, /register, /heartbeat, /drain, /updateAuth, /flush, and /snapshot.

Sandbox / Worktree / Workspace Trust

Recovered modules:

  • ./src/commands/sandbox-config.ts
  • ./src/commands/sandbox-debug.ts
  • ./src/sandbox-gate.ts
  • ./src/utils/sandbox-messages.ts
  • ./src/project/worktree.ts
  • ./src/project/worktree-setup.ts
  • ./src/workspace/approval.tsx
  • ./src/shared/unified-approval-policy.ts

Behavior recovered from source-keyed bundle text:

  • Sandbox settings gate uses feature names composer_sandbox_settings_visible and sandbox_force_disable_win32.
  • Sandbox config has enabled/disabled/reset paths and prints explicit state changes.
  • Workspace trust writes a .workspace-trusted marker under Cursor's project state directory.
  • Workspace trust can approve configured MCP servers when the user chooses the MCP-inclusive trust path.

ACP Modules

Recovered modules under ./src/acp/:

  • acp-storage.ts
  • acp.ts
  • agent-session.ts
  • agent-store.ts
  • config-store.ts
  • cursor-acp-agent.ts
  • interaction-handlers/ask-question-handler.ts
  • interaction-handlers/create-plan-handler.ts
  • resource-link-security.ts
  • run.ts
  • session-list.ts
  • session-resources.ts
  • shared-services.ts
  • streams.ts
  • tool-call-presenters/create-plan-progress.ts
  • types.ts

This supports ACP as a first-class local protocol surface rather than a documentation-only claim.

Remaining Gaps

  • Exact subagent authoring schema and task scheduling.
  • MCP tool invocation inside a real agent/model session. Project approval JSON and standalone tools/list are now traced from sanitized fixtures.
  • Worker bridge auth and frame protocol.
  • Sandbox policy compilation details inside native cursorsandbox.
  • Runtime behavior of worktree setup with dirty files.

015_reverse-engineering/20_synthetic_mcp_config_trace.md

Synthetic MCP Config Trace

This pass used disposable temp HOME/workspace directories and dummy /bin/echo MCP commands. It did not start real MCP servers, use credentials, call model sessions, or read private user config.

Cursor Agent

Synthetic files:

  • temp HOME: <tmp>/home/.cursor/mcp.json
  • temp workspace: <tmp>/ws/.cursor/mcp.json

Config shape used:

{"mcpServers":{"homeDummy":{"command":"/bin/echo","args":["home"]}}}

Findings:

Condition Result
temp workspace .cursor/mcp.json only, non-git directory not discovered
temp HOME ~/.cursor/mcp.json homeDummy: not loaded (needs approval)
temp workspace after git init workspaceDummy: not loaded (needs approval)

Implications:

  • Cursor Agent's synthetic trace supports mcpServers as the config root.
  • User-level ~/.cursor/mcp.json is discovered with temp HOME.
  • Project .cursor/mcp.json discovery appears tied to workspace/git detection in this trace.
  • Servers are not auto-loaded; they require approval.

Follow-up runtime trace:

015_reverse-engineering/29_cr_mcp_handshake_trace.md used a project-scoped .cursor/mcp.json plus a synthetic stdio MCP server. cursor-agent mcp enable trace wrote an approval entry under temp HOME at .cursor/projects/<project-slug>/mcp-approvals.json; the observed entry was trace-0bab3cead8c72734.

mcp list-tools trace then crossed the pre-approval gate and launched the minimal hand-written server, but it timed out without sending any JSON-RPC initialize frame to that fixture.

A second fixture using @modelcontextprotocol/server-everything succeeded: cursor-agent mcp list-tools everything listed 14 tools. This confirms approval enforcement, project-state write behavior, and standalone no-account stdio MCP tool enumeration.

Grok Build

Command:

  • HOME=<tmp>/home ~/.local/bin/grok mcp add dummy --command /bin/echo --args dummy
  • HOME=<tmp>/home ~/.local/bin/grok mcp list --json

Findings:

Surface Result
Add target MCP server 'dummy' saved to ~/.grok/config.toml
JSON list entry includes name, command, args, enabled
Enabled default enabled: true

This confirms that grok mcp add writes user config under temp HOME and that mcp list --json is machine-readable.

Follow-up doctor trace:

015_reverse-engineering/30_grok_mcp_doctor_trace.md configured @modelcontextprotocol/server-everything directly in temp ~/.grok/config.toml. grok mcp doctor everything --json reported command found, server started, handshake OK with protocol 2025-06-18, and 13 tools discovered.

Kiro CLI

Command:

  • HOME=<tmp>/home ~/.local/bin/kiro-cli-chat mcp add --name dummy --scope workspace --command /bin/echo --args dummy --disabled --force
  • HOME=<tmp>/home ~/.local/bin/kiro-cli-chat mcp list
  • HOME=<tmp>/home ~/.local/bin/kiro-cli-chat mcp status --name dummy

Findings:

Surface Result
Write target <tmp>/ws/.kiro/settings/mcp.json
Listed under default agent bucket kiro_default, despite --scope workspace
Status scope Scope: default, Agent: kiro_default
Disabled flag Disabled: true
Default timeout 120000 ms

Implications:

  • Kiro's CLI writes a workspace config file for the trace.
  • In this run, workspace-scoped server display/status resolved under the default agent bucket.
  • Disabled servers are listed and status-inspectable without launching the command.
  • A later enabled official-server status trace in 015_reverse-engineering/24_ki_agent_mcp_trace.md confirmed JSON-array args for @modelcontextprotocol/server-everything, displayed Disabled: false, but still did not report protocol handshake or tool discovery.

AGY CLI

AGY has no public mcp command group in the installed CLI help. No synthetic MCP config trace was run for AGY in this pass. Its MCP evidence remains binary/cache evidence plus public plugin import surface.

Remaining Gaps

  • Cursor MCP project approval behavior and standalone no-account tools/list with an official stdio server are tested in 015_reverse-engineering/29_cr_mcp_handshake_trace.md.
  • Grok native TOML MCP startup, handshake, and tool discovery are tested in 015_reverse-engineering/30_grok_mcp_doctor_trace.md.
  • OAuth flows were not tested.
  • Kiro agent-specific MCP routing with a custom synthetic agent is tested in 015_reverse-engineering/24_ki_agent_mcp_trace.md; actual server startup and protocol handshake remain separate gaps.
  • AGY MCP activation requires either hidden agentapi knowledge, plugin import, or model/session trace.

015_reverse-engineering/24_ki_agent_mcp_trace.md

Kiro Agent MCP Trace

This pass used disposable temp HOME and workspace directories. It did not run model prompts, start real MCP servers, authenticate, or read private Kiro sessions.

Public Help Surface

Commands inspected:

  • ~/.local/bin/kiro-cli-chat agent create --help
  • ~/.local/bin/kiro-cli-chat agent list --help
  • ~/.local/bin/kiro-cli-chat mcp add --help
  • ~/.local/bin/kiro-cli-chat mcp list --help

Relevant help semantics:

  • agent create [NAME] --directory <DIRECTORY> creates an agent config in the chosen directory, but opens an editor by default.
  • agent list says local agents are discovered only when invoked in a directory that contains them.
  • mcp add --agent <AGENT> writes the server to the named agent.
  • mcp add --scope <SCOPE> is only meaningful when no agent name is supplied.

Editor Behavior

agent create trace --directory <tmp>/ws/.kiro/agents created <tmp>/ws/.kiro/agents/trace.json and opened it in vi. The generated template shape was:

{
  "name": "trace",
  "description": "",
  "prompt": null,
  "mcpServers": {},
  "tools": ["*"],
  "toolAliases": {},
  "allowedTools": [],
  "resources": [],
  "hooks": {},
  "toolsSettings": {},
  "includeMcpJson": true,
  "model": null
}

The editor process was terminated after capturing the template. Follow-up traces used a directly written synthetic JSON file with the same shape to avoid interactive editor state.

Workspace Agent Discovery

Synthetic file:

  • <tmp>/ws/.kiro/agents/trace.json

Commands:

  • HOME=<tmp>/home ~/.local/bin/kiro-cli-chat agent validate --path <tmp>/ws/.kiro/agents/trace.json
  • HOME=<tmp>/home ~/.local/bin/kiro-cli-chat agent list

Findings:

Surface Result
validation exit code 0, no output
agent list shows built-ins plus trace as Workspace
built-ins kiro_default, kiro_help, kiro_planner remain visible

Agent-Specific MCP Routing

Command:

  • HOME=<tmp>/home ~/.local/bin/kiro-cli-chat mcp add --name dummy --agent trace --command /bin/echo --args dummy --disabled --force

Observed output:

  • Added MCP server 'dummy' to agent trace

mcp list then grouped the server under workspace agent trace:

workspace:
  kiro_help
    (empty)
  kiro_planner
    (empty)
  trace
    - dummy        /bin/echo (disabled)

mcp status --name dummy reported:

Field Result
Scope workspace
Agent trace
Command /bin/echo
Timeout 120000 ms
Disabled true

The synthetic agent JSON was updated in place:

"mcpServers": {
  "dummy": {
    "command": "/bin/echo",
    "args": ["dummy"],
    "env": {},
    "disabled": true
  }
}

Documentation Consequences

  • Kiro has a public no-model path for validating custom workspace agents and binding MCP servers to a specific agent.
  • --agent <name> overrides --scope; agent-specific MCP config is stored inside the agent JSON rather than the workspace .kiro/settings/mcp.json path observed for non-agent workspace MCP.
  • includeMcpJson: true is present in the generated agent template, but this trace does not prove its runtime inclusion semantics.

Official Server Status Follow-up

A second temp workspace tested an enabled @modelcontextprotocol/server-everything config without a model session:

  • temp HOME: /tmp/kiro-mcp-official.anATMg/home
  • temp workspace: /tmp/kiro-mcp-official.anATMg/ws
  • config file: /tmp/kiro-mcp-official.anATMg/ws/.kiro/settings/mcp.json

Command:

HOME=/tmp/kiro-mcp-official.anATMg/home ~/.local/bin/kiro-cli-chat mcp add --name everything --scope workspace --command ~/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.1/bin/npx --args '["-y","@modelcontextprotocol/server-everything"]' --env npm_config_cache=/tmp/kiro-mcp-official.anATMg/npm-cache --force

Result:

✓ Added MCP server 'everything' to workspace config in /private/tmp/kiro-mcp-official.anATMg/ws/.kiro/settings/mcp.json

The written config used:

{"mcpServers":{"everything":{"command":"~/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.1/bin/npx","args":["-y","@modelcontextprotocol/server-everything"],"env":{"npm_config_cache":"/tmp/kiro-mcp-official.anATMg/npm-cache"}}}}

mcp status --name everything -vv did not report protocol handshake or tool discovery. It reported:

  • not logged in, no valid token found
  • workspace config path /private/tmp/kiro-mcp-official.anATMg/ws/.kiro
  • global config path /tmp/kiro-mcp-official.anATMg/home/.kiro
  • scope displayed as default
  • agent displayed as kiro_default
  • command path to npx
  • timeout 120000 ms
  • disabled false
  • env var npm_config_cache=/tmp/kiro-mcp-official.anATMg/npm-cache

Remaining Gaps

  • Actual MCP server startup and protocol handshake.
  • Agent-specific MCP behavior during a model session.
  • Interaction between includeMcpJson, global/workspace MCP config, and agent-local mcpServers.
  • Custom agent execution, delegation, and tool-permission inheritance.

015_reverse-engineering/29_cr_mcp_handshake_trace.md

Cursor Agent MCP Handshake Trace

June 2026 refresh: 42_cr_mcp_handshake_trace_jun2026.md (cursor-agent 2026.06.26-7079533, 14 tools, 2026-06-28). This file is the May 2026 baseline pass.

This pass used disposable temp HOMEs and temp git workspaces. It did not read private Cursor config, use credentials, call a model session, or mutate the user's real Cursor state.

Minimal Fixture

Synthetic workspace:

  • temp workspace: /tmp/cursor-mcp-final.DLswue/ws
  • temp HOME: /tmp/cursor-mcp-final.DLswue/home
  • config file: /tmp/cursor-mcp-final.DLswue/ws/.cursor/mcp.json
  • approval file written by Cursor Agent: /tmp/cursor-mcp-final.DLswue/home/.cursor/projects/private-tmp-cursor-mcp-final-DLswue-ws/mcp-approvals.json

The config shape was:

{"mcpServers":{"trace":{"command":"~/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.1/bin/node","args":["/tmp/cursor-mcp-final.DLswue/trace-mcp.js"]}}}

The synthetic server was a no-network stdio process that logged startup, parsed Content-Length JSON-RPC frames, answered initialize, and returned one tool from tools/list.

Approval Flow

Command:

HOME=/tmp/cursor-mcp-final.DLswue/home ~/.local/bin/cursor-agent mcp enable trace

from the temp workspace returned:

✓ Enabled and approved MCP server: trace

Cursor Agent wrote:

[
  "trace-0bab3cead8c72734"
]

Implications:

  • mcp enable uses the current working directory, not only --workspace, to resolve project MCP config.
  • Project approval state is stored under temp HOME at .cursor/projects/<project-slug>/mcp-approvals.json.
  • The approval key is derived from the server name, server config, and project root. The trace observed trace-0bab3cead8c72734 for this fixture.

Minimal List Tools Attempt

Command:

HOME=/tmp/cursor-mcp-final.DLswue/home ~/.local/bin/cursor-agent mcp list-tools trace

from the temp workspace returned after a longer timeout:

Failed to list tools: Failed to load MCP 'trace': MCP error -32001: Request timed out
Error: command failed unexpectedly.

The synthetic server log for the same run contained only:

START argv=["~/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.1/bin/node","/tmp/cursor-mcp-final.DLswue/trace-mcp.js"]
STDIN_END
START argv=["~/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.1/bin/node","/tmp/cursor-mcp-final.DLswue/trace-mcp.js"]
STDIN_END

No initialize, notifications/initialized, or tools/list frame reached the server before Cursor Agent closed stdin.

Official MCP Server Success Trace

To separate a fixture implementation issue from Cursor's MCP loader behavior, a second temp workspace used the official @modelcontextprotocol/server-everything package through npx with an isolated npm cache:

  • temp workspace: /tmp/cursor-mcp-official.onq9Rl/ws
  • temp HOME: /tmp/cursor-mcp-official.onq9Rl/home
  • npm cache: /tmp/cursor-mcp-official.onq9Rl/npm-cache
  • config file: /tmp/cursor-mcp-official.onq9Rl/ws/.cursor/mcp.json

Config shape:

{"mcpServers":{"everything":{"command":"~/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.1/bin/npx","args":["-y","@modelcontextprotocol/server-everything"],"env":{"npm_config_cache":"/tmp/cursor-mcp-official.onq9Rl/npm-cache"}}}}

Approval command from the temp workspace returned:

✓ Enabled and approved MCP server: everything

Cursor wrote:

[
  "everything-154da186314e45d5"
]

cursor-agent mcp list-tools everything succeeded and printed 14 tools:

Tools for everything (14):
- echo (message)
- get-annotated-message (messageType, includeImage)
- get-env ()
- get-resource-links (count)
- get-resource-reference (resourceType, resourceId)
- get-structured-content (location)
- get-sum (a, b)
- get-tiny-image ()
- gzip-file-as-resource (name, data, outputType)
- simulate-research-query (topic, ambiguous)
- toggle-simulated-logging ()
- toggle-subscriber-updates ()
- trigger-elicitation-request ()
- trigger-long-running-operation (duration, steps)

Failed Variants

Earlier variants are useful negative controls:

Variant Result
user-level ~/.cursor/mcp.json only trace: not loaded (needs approval)
project config plus --workspace, but command run outside workspace mcp enable trace did not find the server
project config from current workspace before approval mcp list-tools trace failed with MCP server "trace" has not been approved
project config after approval with 20s timeout server started, no protocol frame received, timeout killed the process
project config after approval with 65s timeout server started twice, no protocol frame received, Cursor returned MCP timeout
official @modelcontextprotocol/server-everything after approval mcp list-tools everything succeeded and listed 14 tools

Conclusions

This trace upgrades Cursor MCP evidence from static bundle/config discovery to real approval-file behavior:

  • mcpServers is the operative project config root for .cursor/mcp.json.
  • mcp enable creates project-scoped approval records in the Cursor project-state directory under HOME.
  • mcp list-tools enforces approval before loading a project MCP.
  • The standalone mcp list-tools path can complete a no-account stdio MCP handshake when pointed at a known-good server package.
  • The minimal hand-written stdio fixture still timed out without receiving initialize, so it should not be used as evidence of Cursor protocol failure.

The remaining gap is not basic tools/list; it is deeper runtime behavior inside an actual agent/model session: tool invocation policy, permission prompts, auth/OAuth servers, resource access, and how MCP tools appear in model-facing context.

015_reverse-engineering/30_grok_mcp_doctor_trace.md

Grok MCP Doctor Trace

This pass used a disposable temp HOME and workspace. It did not read private Grok config, use credentials, call a model session, or mutate the user's real Grok state.

Fixture

  • temp HOME: /tmp/grok-mcp-doctor3.jsPmH7/home
  • temp workspace: /tmp/grok-mcp-doctor3.jsPmH7/ws
  • isolated npm cache: /tmp/grok-mcp-doctor3.jsPmH7/npm-cache
  • config file: /tmp/grok-mcp-doctor3.jsPmH7/home/.grok/config.toml

Config:

[mcp_servers.everything]
command = "~/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.1/bin/npx"
args = ["-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-everything"]
env = { npm_config_cache = "/tmp/grok-mcp-doctor3.jsPmH7/npm-cache" }
enabled = true

Doctor Result

Command:

HOME=/tmp/grok-mcp-doctor3.jsPmH7/home ~/.local/bin/grok mcp doctor everything --json

Result summary:

{
  "healthy_count": 1,
  "failing_count": 0,
  "servers": [
    {
      "name": "everything",
      "transport": "stdio",
      "source": "config",
      "healthy": true
    }
  ]
}

Checks reported by Grok:

Check Result
command found passed, ~/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.1/bin/npx
server started passed, 0.0s
handshake OK passed, protocol 2025-06-18
tools discovered passed, 13 tools discovered

Source discovery in the same JSON:

Source Status
~/.grok/config.toml found, server_count: 1
~/.claude.json not found
.mcp.json not found
grok.com skipped, reason not logged in

CLI Add Negative Control

Two attempts to configure this server through grok mcp add failed because --args -y ... was parsed as an unexpected CLI option:

error: unexpected argument '-y' found
tip: to pass '-y' as a value, use '-- -y'

Earlier simple traces confirmed grok mcp add dummy --command /bin/echo --args dummy writes to temp ~/.grok/config.toml; this negative control is specific to leading-dash argument passing for npx -y.

Conclusions

  • Grok's native TOML mcp_servers.<name> config can launch a real stdio MCP server in a no-account temp HOME.
  • grok mcp doctor --json performs protocol-level startup validation, not just static config parsing.
  • The doctor path confirms protocol 2025-06-18 and tool discovery for a known-good MCP server.
  • Remote source discovery includes grok.com, but it is skipped without login in this trace.
  • Remaining MCP gaps are model-session tool invocation, OAuth/HTTP flows, project precedence layering, and plugin-provided MCP activation.

015_reverse-engineering/42_cr_mcp_handshake_trace_jun2026.md

Cursor Agent MCP Handshake Trace — June 2026 Bundle

Re-run of 29_cr_mcp_handshake_trace.md against installed cursor-agent 2026.06.26-7079533.
Disposable temp HOME and workspace only — no real Cursor credentials, no model session, no user ~/.cursor reads.

Version pin: 36_installed_cli_version_manifest.md

Binary under test

Field Value
Launcher ~/.local/bin/cursor-agent → 2026.06.26-7079533/cursor-agent

Official MCP server fixture

Field Value

Workspace /tmp/cursor-mcp-jun2026.6zD201/ws
Temp HOME /tmp/cursor-mcp-jun2026.6zD201/home
npm cache /tmp/cursor-mcp-jun2026.6zD201/npm-cache

{"mcpServers":{"everything":{"command":"~/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.1/bin/npx","args":["-y","@modelcontextprotocol/server-everything"],"env":{"npm_config_cache":"/tmp/cursor-mcp-jun2026.6zD201/npm-cache"}}}}

Output (2026-06-28, completed within 90s):

Tools for everything (14):
- echo (message)

Conclusions (June bundle)

Claim Status on 2026.06.26-7079533
Project .cursor/mcp.json is operative config root ✅ confirmed (same as May)
mcp enable writes project-scoped approval under temp HOME ✅ confirmed
mcp list-tools requires approval before load ✅ confirmed
No-auth stdio handshake with official server-everything ✅ 14 tools listed
Hand-written minimal stdio fixture not re-run; May negative controls still apply

Parity with May pass: handshake behavior unchanged on June bundle; tool count 14 matches 29.

Remaining gap (unchanged)

Runtime behavior inside an authenticated model/agent session (tool invocation policy, OAuth MCP, resources in model context) is still out of scope.

Related

  • May baseline trace: 29_cr_mcp_handshake_trace.md
  • RE hub: 15_cr_reverse.md

Korean corpus (reference)

015_reverse-engineering/00_reverse_evidence_policy.md

Reverse Engineering Evidence Policy

이 축은 설치 help parity 문서의 다음 단계다. 기존 00~14 축은 주로 help/config/local-doc 표면을 비교했고, 이 축은 설치된 바이너리와 번들에서 복원 가능한 구조를 따로 기록한다.

Evidence Levels

Level 의미 사용할 수 있는 주장
installed-help --help, public command output, version output 명령/플래그/공개 subcommand가 존재한다
safe-local-doc 설치 패키지의 docs, changelog, examples, manifest 문서화된 schema/경로/기능 설명이 있다
bundle-key minified bundle에 남은 module key, source path, route name, proto name 내부 모듈/라우터/프로토/컴포넌트가 존재한다
binary-string native binary strings, file, otool, codesign, nm crate/package/protocol/tool/evaluator 이름이 embedded되어 있다
runtime-trace synthetic project에서 실제 명령을 실행한 결과 동작 semantics, precedence, permission enforcement를 확인했다
private-artifact-name 비공개 상태 파일의 경로/개수만 관찰 artifact가 존재한다. 내용/schema 주장은 금지

015_reverse-engineering/33_source_deobfuscation_inventory.md further separates source-like recovery levels. In this corpus, Cursor reaches preserved module-key recovery, while Grok/Kiro/AGY mostly expose native source-path, handler, protobuf, and runtime-trace evidence. Do not call this "full source recovered" unless a real source map or readable source archive is present.

Hard Boundary

역분석의 목적은 제품 구조 복원이지 사용자 상태 추출이 아니다. 다음은 내용 읽기 금지다.

  • auth/token/cookie/credential/login data
  • raw transcript, chat history, conversation DB/PB
  • logs and terminal captures
  • browser profile state, local storage, IndexedDB, session storage
  • memory/goal runtime stores
  • screenshots and browser recordings
  • private mcp.json, settings, config values unless explicitly redacted

비공개 artifact는 “존재한다”까지만 쓴다. 필드/schema/content 주장은 public docs, source/bundle evidence, sanitized fixture, synthetic runtime trace가 있을 때만 쓴다.

Installed CLI version pin (2026-06-27)

Single source of truth for pinned ay/cr/gr/ki binaries:

  • 015_reverse-engineering/36_installed_cli_version_manifest.md

WP5 trace shards (38–41) extend hubs without changing evidence-level definitions above.

Current Upgrade Scope

2026-06-27 pass — installed CLI practical RE (goal 4e94e12d-a9b):

Prefix Target Evidence kind
ay ~/.local/bin/agy v1.0.12+ help, changelog, strings; 38_ay_project_flags_trace.md
cr cursor-agent 2026.06.26-7079533 bundle string literals, help; 37, 39_cr_automations_help_trace.md
gr grok-0.2.67-macos-aarch64 inspect JSON, strings; 40_gr_0267_inspect_refresh.md
ki Kiro CLI.app 2.5.0 help surfaces; 41_ki_help_surface_trace.md

2026-05-31 pass에서 새로 역분석한 대상 (historical baseline):

Prefix Target Evidence kind
gr ~/.grok/downloads/grok-0.2.14-macos-aarch64, ~/.grok/bundled, ~/.grok/docs/user-guide Rust Mach-O strings, crate paths, ACP methods, bundled agents/roles/personas/skills
cr ~/.local/share/cursor-agent/versions/2026.05.28-418efe5 Webpack module keys, route extraction, proto module names, native helper inventory
ki /Applications/Kiro CLI.app, ~/.local/bin/kiro-cli* signed universal Mach-O binaries, Rust source-path strings, WebKit resources, dashboard/autocomplete assets
ay ~/.local/bin/agy, ~/.gemini/antigravity-cli Go/native strings, protobuf trajectory/cascade names, local cache/config metadata, helper binaries

Reading Rule For Existing Docs

When a 00~14 page says “없음” or “지원하지 않음”, interpret it as “installed help에서 노출되지 않음” unless this 15_reverse-engineering axis confirms absence from bundle/binary evidence too.

When a page states runtime semantics from config/cache observation, this axis upgrades or downgrades the claim:

  • bundle-key or binary-string confirms a module exists.
  • runtime-trace is still required for exact precedence, scheduling, isolation, merge, and permission behavior.

015_reverse-engineering/25_cr_bundle_module_map.md

Cursor Agent Bundle Module Map

Current install: 37_cr_bundle_refresh_20260626.md + 36_installed_cli_version_manifest.md (2026.06.26-7079533). This page is the May 2026 baseline for webpack exports.modules recovery.

This pass inspected installed Cursor Agent JavaScript bundles under ~/.local/share/cursor-agent/versions/2026.05.28-418efe5. It did not execute model sessions, authenticate, read private Cursor data, or mutate Cursor config.

Bundle Identity

Field Evidence
Package root ~/.local/share/cursor-agent/versions/2026.05.28-418efe5
Package name @anysphere/agent-cli-runtime
Release marker agent-cli@2026.05.28-418efe5 in Sentry release strings
Source maps none present as *.map
Module recovery method Webpack exports.modules keys preserved original ./src/... paths
Recovered ./src/ module keys 408 unique keys; 479 key occurrences across chunks

Launchers and binaries:

  • cursor-agent: shell launcher
  • cursor-agent-svc: shell launcher
  • cursor-agent-svc.js: local service bundle
  • index.js: main CLI bundle
  • node: bundled Mach-O arm64 Node runtime
  • cursorsandbox: Mach-O arm64 sandbox helper
  • native addons: file_service.darwin-arm64.node, merkle-tree-napi.darwin-arm64.node, node_sqlite3.node, pty.node

Command Module Map

The bundle exposes 53 unique command modules under ./src/commands/. High-signal groups:

Group Recovered modules
Chat/session chat.ts, create-chat.ts, resume.tsx, cloud-list.ts, cloud-transcript.ts
MCP mcp-list.ts, mcp-list-tools.ts, mcp-login.ts, enable.ts, disable.ts
Sandbox sandbox-config.ts, sandbox-debug.ts
Worker worker-mode.ts, worker-pool.ts, worker-debug.ts, worker-directory.ts, worker-account-policy.ts, worker-policy-validation.ts
Auth/env login.ts, logout.ts, auth-status-format.ts, dev-login.ts, env.ts
ACP acp.ts
Search/record/status search.ts, record.ts, status.ts, repo-status.tsx, repo-info.tsx
Install/update install.ts, install-core-posix.ts, install-shell-integration.ts, update.ts, update-core.ts

This confirms the CLI command surface is not a thin wrapper around agent [prompt...]; it is a multi-command runtime with worker, MCP, sandbox, auth, cloud, and ACP subcommands.

Subagent And Collaboration Modules

Recovered modules:

  • ./src/subagent/cli-subagent-host-adapter.ts
  • ./src/subagent/subagent-prompt-handler.ts
  • ./src/subagent/subagent-session-store.ts
  • ./src/subagent/subagent-token-store.ts
  • ./src/subagent/subagent-update-router.ts
  • ./src/context/subagent-session-context.tsx
  • ./src/shared/cli-subagent-host-adapter.ts
  • generated proto import family: subagent_exec_pb.js

Implication: Cursor Agent has a real subagent host/session/update stack in the installed bundle. Public help still lacks a standalone subagent command group, so exact authoring and execution semantics remain runtime-trace pending.

MCP And Approval Modules

Recovered modules:

  • ./src/mcp/approval.tsx
  • ./src/mcp/approve-plugin-mcps-on-install.ts
  • ./src/mcp/disabled-store.ts
  • ./src/mcp/error-utils.ts
  • ./src/mcp/project-paths.ts
  • ./src/mcp/scope.ts
  • ./src/shared/lazy-mcp-lease.ts
  • UI modules for MCP tools/resources/servers under ./src/components/*mcp*

Key behavior recovered from bundle snippets:

  • User config path: ~/.cursor/mcp.json
  • Project config path: <projectRoot>/.cursor/mcp.json
  • State files: MCP approvals and disabled-store paths are derived through project-paths.ts
  • Project MCP enable flow computes approval IDs for project servers and removes disabled entries
  • OAuth callback helper listens on localhost port 8787 and verifies callback state
  • Error mapping distinguishes disabled servers, team policy blocks, and team network allowlist blocks

Sanitized runtime trace in 015_reverse-engineering/29_cr_mcp_handshake_trace.md confirms the approval path at runtime: project mcp enable writes .cursor/projects/<project-slug>/mcp-approvals.json under HOME, mcp list-tools rejects unapproved project servers before loading them, and an official stdio MCP server can be loaded enough to enumerate 14 tools.

Worker / Bridge / Service Modules

Recovered modules:

  • ./src/bridge/bridge.ts
  • ./src/bridge/bridge-client.ts
  • ./src/bridge/management-server.ts
  • ./src/bridge/worker-api-server.ts
  • ./src/bridge/proxy-http2-session-manager.ts
  • ./src/bridge/self-hosted-worker-metrics.ts
  • ./src/commands/worker-mode.ts
  • ./src/commands/worker-pool.ts
  • ./src/commands/worker-directory.ts
  • ./src/commands/worker-debug.ts

Management-server routes recovered from source-keyed bundle text:

  • /healthz
  • /readyz
  • optional /metrics

This is distinct from cursor-agent-svc.js local service routes already documented as /health, /register, /heartbeat, /drain, /updateAuth, /flush, and /snapshot.

Sandbox / Worktree / Workspace Trust

Recovered modules:

  • ./src/commands/sandbox-config.ts
  • ./src/commands/sandbox-debug.ts
  • ./src/sandbox-gate.ts
  • ./src/utils/sandbox-messages.ts
  • ./src/project/worktree.ts
  • ./src/project/worktree-setup.ts
  • ./src/workspace/approval.tsx
  • ./src/shared/unified-approval-policy.ts

Behavior recovered from source-keyed bundle text:

  • Sandbox settings gate uses feature names composer_sandbox_settings_visible and sandbox_force_disable_win32.
  • Sandbox config has enabled/disabled/reset paths and prints explicit state changes.
  • Workspace trust writes a .workspace-trusted marker under Cursor's project state directory.
  • Workspace trust can approve configured MCP servers when the user chooses the MCP-inclusive trust path.

ACP Modules

Recovered modules under ./src/acp/:

  • acp-storage.ts
  • acp.ts
  • agent-session.ts
  • agent-store.ts
  • config-store.ts
  • cursor-acp-agent.ts
  • interaction-handlers/ask-question-handler.ts
  • interaction-handlers/create-plan-handler.ts
  • resource-link-security.ts
  • run.ts
  • session-list.ts
  • session-resources.ts
  • shared-services.ts
  • streams.ts
  • tool-call-presenters/create-plan-progress.ts
  • types.ts

This supports ACP as a first-class local protocol surface rather than a documentation-only claim.

Remaining Gaps

  • Exact subagent authoring schema and task scheduling.
  • MCP tool invocation inside a real agent/model session. Project approval JSON and standalone tools/list are now traced from sanitized fixtures.
  • Worker bridge auth and frame protocol.
  • Sandbox policy compilation details inside native cursorsandbox.
  • Runtime behavior of worktree setup with dirty files.

015_reverse-engineering/20_synthetic_mcp_config_trace.md

Synthetic MCP Config Trace

This pass used disposable temp HOME/workspace directories and dummy /bin/echo MCP commands. It did not start real MCP servers, use credentials, call model sessions, or read private user config.

Cursor Agent

Synthetic files:

  • temp HOME: <tmp>/home/.cursor/mcp.json
  • temp workspace: <tmp>/ws/.cursor/mcp.json

Config shape used:

{"mcpServers":{"homeDummy":{"command":"/bin/echo","args":["home"]}}}

Findings:

Condition Result
temp workspace .cursor/mcp.json only, non-git directory not discovered
temp HOME ~/.cursor/mcp.json homeDummy: not loaded (needs approval)
temp workspace after git init workspaceDummy: not loaded (needs approval)

Implications:

  • Cursor Agent's synthetic trace supports mcpServers as the config root.
  • User-level ~/.cursor/mcp.json is discovered with temp HOME.
  • Project .cursor/mcp.json discovery appears tied to workspace/git detection in this trace.
  • Servers are not auto-loaded; they require approval.

Follow-up runtime trace:

015_reverse-engineering/29_cr_mcp_handshake_trace.md used a project-scoped .cursor/mcp.json plus a synthetic stdio MCP server. cursor-agent mcp enable trace wrote an approval entry under temp HOME at .cursor/projects/<project-slug>/mcp-approvals.json; the observed entry was trace-0bab3cead8c72734.

mcp list-tools trace then crossed the pre-approval gate and launched the minimal hand-written server, but it timed out without sending any JSON-RPC initialize frame to that fixture.

A second fixture using @modelcontextprotocol/server-everything succeeded: cursor-agent mcp list-tools everything listed 14 tools. This confirms approval enforcement, project-state write behavior, and standalone no-account stdio MCP tool enumeration.

Grok Build

Command:

  • HOME=<tmp>/home ~/.local/bin/grok mcp add dummy --command /bin/echo --args dummy
  • HOME=<tmp>/home ~/.local/bin/grok mcp list --json

Findings:

Surface Result
Add target MCP server 'dummy' saved to ~/.grok/config.toml
JSON list entry includes name, command, args, enabled
Enabled default enabled: true

This confirms that grok mcp add writes user config under temp HOME and that mcp list --json is machine-readable.

Follow-up doctor trace:

015_reverse-engineering/30_grok_mcp_doctor_trace.md configured @modelcontextprotocol/server-everything directly in temp ~/.grok/config.toml. grok mcp doctor everything --json reported command found, server started, handshake OK with protocol 2025-06-18, and 13 tools discovered.

Kiro CLI

Command:

  • HOME=<tmp>/home ~/.local/bin/kiro-cli-chat mcp add --name dummy --scope workspace --command /bin/echo --args dummy --disabled --force
  • HOME=<tmp>/home ~/.local/bin/kiro-cli-chat mcp list
  • HOME=<tmp>/home ~/.local/bin/kiro-cli-chat mcp status --name dummy

Findings:

Surface Result
Write target <tmp>/ws/.kiro/settings/mcp.json
Listed under default agent bucket kiro_default, despite --scope workspace
Status scope Scope: default, Agent: kiro_default
Disabled flag Disabled: true
Default timeout 120000 ms

Implications:

  • Kiro's CLI writes a workspace config file for the trace.
  • In this run, workspace-scoped server display/status resolved under the default agent bucket.
  • Disabled servers are listed and status-inspectable without launching the command.
  • A later enabled official-server status trace in 015_reverse-engineering/24_ki_agent_mcp_trace.md confirmed JSON-array args for @modelcontextprotocol/server-everything, displayed Disabled: false, but still did not report protocol handshake or tool discovery.

AGY CLI

AGY has no public mcp command group in the installed CLI help. No synthetic MCP config trace was run for AGY in this pass. Its MCP evidence remains binary/cache evidence plus public plugin import surface.

Remaining Gaps

  • Cursor MCP project approval behavior and standalone no-account tools/list with an official stdio server are tested in 015_reverse-engineering/29_cr_mcp_handshake_trace.md.
  • Grok native TOML MCP startup, handshake, and tool discovery are tested in 015_reverse-engineering/30_grok_mcp_doctor_trace.md.
  • OAuth flows were not tested.
  • Kiro agent-specific MCP routing with a custom synthetic agent is tested in 015_reverse-engineering/24_ki_agent_mcp_trace.md; actual server startup and protocol handshake remain separate gaps.
  • AGY MCP activation requires either hidden agentapi knowledge, plugin import, or model/session trace.

015_reverse-engineering/24_ki_agent_mcp_trace.md

Kiro Agent MCP Trace

This pass used disposable temp HOME and workspace directories. It did not run model prompts, start real MCP servers, authenticate, or read private Kiro sessions.

Public Help Surface

Commands inspected:

  • ~/.local/bin/kiro-cli-chat agent create --help
  • ~/.local/bin/kiro-cli-chat agent list --help
  • ~/.local/bin/kiro-cli-chat mcp add --help
  • ~/.local/bin/kiro-cli-chat mcp list --help

Relevant help semantics:

  • agent create [NAME] --directory <DIRECTORY> creates an agent config in the chosen directory, but opens an editor by default.
  • agent list says local agents are discovered only when invoked in a directory that contains them.
  • mcp add --agent <AGENT> writes the server to the named agent.
  • mcp add --scope <SCOPE> is only meaningful when no agent name is supplied.

Editor Behavior

agent create trace --directory <tmp>/ws/.kiro/agents created <tmp>/ws/.kiro/agents/trace.json and opened it in vi. The generated template shape was:

{
  "name": "trace",
  "description": "",
  "prompt": null,
  "mcpServers": {},
  "tools": ["*"],
  "toolAliases": {},
  "allowedTools": [],
  "resources": [],
  "hooks": {},
  "toolsSettings": {},
  "includeMcpJson": true,
  "model": null
}

The editor process was terminated after capturing the template. Follow-up traces used a directly written synthetic JSON file with the same shape to avoid interactive editor state.

Workspace Agent Discovery

Synthetic file:

  • <tmp>/ws/.kiro/agents/trace.json

Commands:

  • HOME=<tmp>/home ~/.local/bin/kiro-cli-chat agent validate --path <tmp>/ws/.kiro/agents/trace.json
  • HOME=<tmp>/home ~/.local/bin/kiro-cli-chat agent list

Findings:

Surface Result
validation exit code 0, no output
agent list shows built-ins plus trace as Workspace
built-ins kiro_default, kiro_help, kiro_planner remain visible

Agent-Specific MCP Routing

Command:

  • HOME=<tmp>/home ~/.local/bin/kiro-cli-chat mcp add --name dummy --agent trace --command /bin/echo --args dummy --disabled --force

Observed output:

  • Added MCP server 'dummy' to agent trace

mcp list then grouped the server under workspace agent trace:

workspace:
  kiro_help
    (empty)
  kiro_planner
    (empty)
  trace
    - dummy        /bin/echo (disabled)

mcp status --name dummy reported:

Field Result
Scope workspace
Agent trace
Command /bin/echo
Timeout 120000 ms
Disabled true

The synthetic agent JSON was updated in place:

"mcpServers": {
  "dummy": {
    "command": "/bin/echo",
    "args": ["dummy"],
    "env": {},
    "disabled": true
  }
}

Documentation Consequences

  • Kiro has a public no-model path for validating custom workspace agents and binding MCP servers to a specific agent.
  • --agent <name> overrides --scope; agent-specific MCP config is stored inside the agent JSON rather than the workspace .kiro/settings/mcp.json path observed for non-agent workspace MCP.
  • includeMcpJson: true is present in the generated agent template, but this trace does not prove its runtime inclusion semantics.

Official Server Status Follow-up

A second temp workspace tested an enabled @modelcontextprotocol/server-everything config without a model session:

  • temp HOME: /tmp/kiro-mcp-official.anATMg/home
  • temp workspace: /tmp/kiro-mcp-official.anATMg/ws
  • config file: /tmp/kiro-mcp-official.anATMg/ws/.kiro/settings/mcp.json

Command:

HOME=/tmp/kiro-mcp-official.anATMg/home ~/.local/bin/kiro-cli-chat mcp add --name everything --scope workspace --command ~/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.1/bin/npx --args '["-y","@modelcontextprotocol/server-everything"]' --env npm_config_cache=/tmp/kiro-mcp-official.anATMg/npm-cache --force

Result:

✓ Added MCP server 'everything' to workspace config in /private/tmp/kiro-mcp-official.anATMg/ws/.kiro/settings/mcp.json

The written config used:

{"mcpServers":{"everything":{"command":"~/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.1/bin/npx","args":["-y","@modelcontextprotocol/server-everything"],"env":{"npm_config_cache":"/tmp/kiro-mcp-official.anATMg/npm-cache"}}}}

mcp status --name everything -vv did not report protocol handshake or tool discovery. It reported:

  • not logged in, no valid token found
  • workspace config path /private/tmp/kiro-mcp-official.anATMg/ws/.kiro
  • global config path /tmp/kiro-mcp-official.anATMg/home/.kiro
  • scope displayed as default
  • agent displayed as kiro_default
  • command path to npx
  • timeout 120000 ms
  • disabled false
  • env var npm_config_cache=/tmp/kiro-mcp-official.anATMg/npm-cache

Remaining Gaps

  • Actual MCP server startup and protocol handshake.
  • Agent-specific MCP behavior during a model session.
  • Interaction between includeMcpJson, global/workspace MCP config, and agent-local mcpServers.
  • Custom agent execution, delegation, and tool-permission inheritance.

015_reverse-engineering/29_cr_mcp_handshake_trace.md

Cursor Agent MCP Handshake Trace

June 2026 refresh: 42_cr_mcp_handshake_trace_jun2026.md (cursor-agent 2026.06.26-7079533, 14 tools, 2026-06-28). This file is the May 2026 baseline pass.

This pass used disposable temp HOMEs and temp git workspaces. It did not read private Cursor config, use credentials, call a model session, or mutate the user's real Cursor state.

Minimal Fixture

Synthetic workspace:

  • temp workspace: /tmp/cursor-mcp-final.DLswue/ws
  • temp HOME: /tmp/cursor-mcp-final.DLswue/home
  • config file: /tmp/cursor-mcp-final.DLswue/ws/.cursor/mcp.json
  • approval file written by Cursor Agent: /tmp/cursor-mcp-final.DLswue/home/.cursor/projects/private-tmp-cursor-mcp-final-DLswue-ws/mcp-approvals.json

The config shape was:

{"mcpServers":{"trace":{"command":"~/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.1/bin/node","args":["/tmp/cursor-mcp-final.DLswue/trace-mcp.js"]}}}

The synthetic server was a no-network stdio process that logged startup, parsed Content-Length JSON-RPC frames, answered initialize, and returned one tool from tools/list.

Approval Flow

Command:

HOME=/tmp/cursor-mcp-final.DLswue/home ~/.local/bin/cursor-agent mcp enable trace

from the temp workspace returned:

✓ Enabled and approved MCP server: trace

Cursor Agent wrote:

[
  "trace-0bab3cead8c72734"
]

Implications:

  • mcp enable uses the current working directory, not only --workspace, to resolve project MCP config.
  • Project approval state is stored under temp HOME at .cursor/projects/<project-slug>/mcp-approvals.json.
  • The approval key is derived from the server name, server config, and project root. The trace observed trace-0bab3cead8c72734 for this fixture.

Minimal List Tools Attempt

Command:

HOME=/tmp/cursor-mcp-final.DLswue/home ~/.local/bin/cursor-agent mcp list-tools trace

from the temp workspace returned after a longer timeout:

Failed to list tools: Failed to load MCP 'trace': MCP error -32001: Request timed out
Error: command failed unexpectedly.

The synthetic server log for the same run contained only:

START argv=["~/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.1/bin/node","/tmp/cursor-mcp-final.DLswue/trace-mcp.js"]
STDIN_END
START argv=["~/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.1/bin/node","/tmp/cursor-mcp-final.DLswue/trace-mcp.js"]
STDIN_END

No initialize, notifications/initialized, or tools/list frame reached the server before Cursor Agent closed stdin.

Official MCP Server Success Trace

To separate a fixture implementation issue from Cursor's MCP loader behavior, a second temp workspace used the official @modelcontextprotocol/server-everything package through npx with an isolated npm cache:

  • temp workspace: /tmp/cursor-mcp-official.onq9Rl/ws
  • temp HOME: /tmp/cursor-mcp-official.onq9Rl/home
  • npm cache: /tmp/cursor-mcp-official.onq9Rl/npm-cache
  • config file: /tmp/cursor-mcp-official.onq9Rl/ws/.cursor/mcp.json

Config shape:

{"mcpServers":{"everything":{"command":"~/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.1/bin/npx","args":["-y","@modelcontextprotocol/server-everything"],"env":{"npm_config_cache":"/tmp/cursor-mcp-official.onq9Rl/npm-cache"}}}}

Approval command from the temp workspace returned:

✓ Enabled and approved MCP server: everything

Cursor wrote:

[
  "everything-154da186314e45d5"
]

cursor-agent mcp list-tools everything succeeded and printed 14 tools:

Tools for everything (14):
- echo (message)
- get-annotated-message (messageType, includeImage)
- get-env ()
- get-resource-links (count)
- get-resource-reference (resourceType, resourceId)
- get-structured-content (location)
- get-sum (a, b)
- get-tiny-image ()
- gzip-file-as-resource (name, data, outputType)
- simulate-research-query (topic, ambiguous)
- toggle-simulated-logging ()
- toggle-subscriber-updates ()
- trigger-elicitation-request ()
- trigger-long-running-operation (duration, steps)

Failed Variants

Earlier variants are useful negative controls:

Variant Result
user-level ~/.cursor/mcp.json only trace: not loaded (needs approval)
project config plus --workspace, but command run outside workspace mcp enable trace did not find the server
project config from current workspace before approval mcp list-tools trace failed with MCP server "trace" has not been approved
project config after approval with 20s timeout server started, no protocol frame received, timeout killed the process
project config after approval with 65s timeout server started twice, no protocol frame received, Cursor returned MCP timeout
official @modelcontextprotocol/server-everything after approval mcp list-tools everything succeeded and listed 14 tools

Conclusions

This trace upgrades Cursor MCP evidence from static bundle/config discovery to real approval-file behavior:

  • mcpServers is the operative project config root for .cursor/mcp.json.
  • mcp enable creates project-scoped approval records in the Cursor project-state directory under HOME.
  • mcp list-tools enforces approval before loading a project MCP.
  • The standalone mcp list-tools path can complete a no-account stdio MCP handshake when pointed at a known-good server package.
  • The minimal hand-written stdio fixture still timed out without receiving initialize, so it should not be used as evidence of Cursor protocol failure.

The remaining gap is not basic tools/list; it is deeper runtime behavior inside an actual agent/model session: tool invocation policy, permission prompts, auth/OAuth servers, resource access, and how MCP tools appear in model-facing context.

015_reverse-engineering/30_grok_mcp_doctor_trace.md

Grok MCP Doctor Trace

This pass used a disposable temp HOME and workspace. It did not read private Grok config, use credentials, call a model session, or mutate the user's real Grok state.

Fixture

  • temp HOME: /tmp/grok-mcp-doctor3.jsPmH7/home
  • temp workspace: /tmp/grok-mcp-doctor3.jsPmH7/ws
  • isolated npm cache: /tmp/grok-mcp-doctor3.jsPmH7/npm-cache
  • config file: /tmp/grok-mcp-doctor3.jsPmH7/home/.grok/config.toml

Config:

[mcp_servers.everything]
command = "~/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.1/bin/npx"
args = ["-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-everything"]
env = { npm_config_cache = "/tmp/grok-mcp-doctor3.jsPmH7/npm-cache" }
enabled = true

Doctor Result

Command:

HOME=/tmp/grok-mcp-doctor3.jsPmH7/home ~/.local/bin/grok mcp doctor everything --json

Result summary:

{
  "healthy_count": 1,
  "failing_count": 0,
  "servers": [
    {
      "name": "everything",
      "transport": "stdio",
      "source": "config",
      "healthy": true
    }
  ]
}

Checks reported by Grok:

Check Result
command found passed, ~/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.1/bin/npx
server started passed, 0.0s
handshake OK passed, protocol 2025-06-18
tools discovered passed, 13 tools discovered

Source discovery in the same JSON:

Source Status
~/.grok/config.toml found, server_count: 1
~/.claude.json not found
.mcp.json not found
grok.com skipped, reason not logged in

CLI Add Negative Control

Two attempts to configure this server through grok mcp add failed because --args -y ... was parsed as an unexpected CLI option:

error: unexpected argument '-y' found
tip: to pass '-y' as a value, use '-- -y'

Earlier simple traces confirmed grok mcp add dummy --command /bin/echo --args dummy writes to temp ~/.grok/config.toml; this negative control is specific to leading-dash argument passing for npx -y.

Conclusions

  • Grok's native TOML mcp_servers.<name> config can launch a real stdio MCP server in a no-account temp HOME.
  • grok mcp doctor --json performs protocol-level startup validation, not just static config parsing.
  • The doctor path confirms protocol 2025-06-18 and tool discovery for a known-good MCP server.
  • Remote source discovery includes grok.com, but it is skipped without login in this trace.
  • Remaining MCP gaps are model-session tool invocation, OAuth/HTTP flows, project precedence layering, and plugin-provided MCP activation.

Conclusions (June bundle)

Claim Status on 2026.06.26-7079533
Project .cursor/mcp.json is operative config root ✅ confirmed (same as May)
mcp enable writes project-scoped approval under temp HOME ✅ confirmed
mcp list-tools requires approval before load ✅ confirmed
No-auth stdio handshake with official server-everything ✅ 14 tools listed
Hand-written minimal stdio fixture not re-run; May negative controls still apply

Parity with May pass: handshake behavior unchanged on June bundle; tool count 14 matches 29.

Remaining gap (unchanged)

Runtime behavior inside an authenticated model/agent session (tool invocation policy, OAuth MCP, resources in model context) is still out of scope.

Technical reference

scouting + synthesis

Overview

Technical reference & synthesis — developer-depth section from the codex corpus. Structural highlights extracted from the analysis:

  • 2026-03-26: Copilot CLI 역공학 문서를 완전한 역공학 설명 형식으로 대폭 확장. 번들 분석 개요(520K줄 디컴파일 파이프라인), 3단 부트스트랩, APCA 색상 과학, BasicTruncator 3단계 + CompactionProcessor 비동기 압축, ACP 30+ Zod 프로토콜, 7종 Permission Kinds, 5개 YAML 에이전트 정의(실제 yaml 증거 포함), promptParts 5
  • 이 문서는 Codex, Aider, Gemini CLI, OpenCode, Claude Code를 13개 아키텍처 차원에서 동시에 비교한 캡스톤 문서다.
  • graph TD
  • A[5개 에이전트 개요] --> B[13개 차원 비교]

  • D --> E
  • E --> F[미래 전망과 설계 교훈]
  • 따라서 여기서는 TUI, prompt, tool, skill, subagent, app-server, sandbox, config, MCP, memory, plugin, guardian, 그리고 잔여 교차 관심사를 하나의 프레임으로 다시 묶는다.
  • 근거: [[00_co_overview]], [[00_ai_overview]], [[00_ge_overview]], [[00_oc_overview]], [[00_cc_overview]]
